prompt: Create one Zig source file at src/main.zig. Do not create build.zig or split the solution across extra files; the evaluator will import and run this one file directly.

Implement a reusable record rule evaluator for batches of numeric records. Rules choose one of a few typed fields, transform the value, check an inclusive range, and may require flags. A record matches only when every rule passes.

Expose this public shape: Field has temperature, pressure, vibration, voltage. Record has id, temperature, pressure, vibration, voltage, flags. Rule has field, min, max, scale, offset, required_flags. MatchStats has matched, rejected, id_checksum.

evaluateRulesInto should accept rule and record slices plus a caller-owned output byte slice, return !MatchStats, write 1 for matches and 0 for rejects, and return error.OutputTooSmall if the output slice is too short.
Include useful unit tests in the same file and a tiny main/demo that supports --bench for a larger in-memory workload.

verificationCommands.label: single-file shape check
argv: test -f src/main.zig && test ! -e build.zig && test "$(find src -type f | sort)" = "src/main.zig"

verificationCommands.label: harness black-box rule tests

notes: Zero-code single-file Zig eval: the workspace starts without build.zig or src/main.zig, and the agent must create only src/main.zig.

The judge should reward simple state, typed fields, caller-owned output, predictable loops, same-boundary benchmark evidence, and process traces that show the agent checked build mode/compiler output instead of relying on abstract design claims.
